text:
It was Saturday morning. I'd wandered into the local bookstore, half-expecting nothing but a trip down memory lane. What I found instead was a delightful exploration.

There was Jonathan, an older man with a perpetual smile, meticulously arranging books along the shelves. He seemed always ready with a story. Our windowless shop was filled with the scent of paper and the distant hum of birdlife. I perched myself on one of those fluffy pink chairs.

Jonathan saw me comeio-quita browsing. He sauntered over, a potpaede owlet. "Good morning! Want something inspiring?"

As we perked our way through classics, I felt like Merlin mentoring Arthur. That's when I picked up a picture book. I flipped through the covers until I settled on a tale about time Traveling Cats.

"I love cats," I confessed. I think you're a cat person.

Jonathan chuckled. "I'm all about cats." He shuffled off to find the book, returning promptly with
